title: Hamas Refuses Disarmament Talks Until Israel Fulfills Phase 1 Obligations
detail: On April 3, 2026, Hamas formally reiterated its refusal to discuss disarmament under the Board of Peace framework until Israel fulfills all obligations under Phase 1 of the Trump Gaza plan, including a full withdrawal from populated areas and completion of all prisoner exchanges. Hamas officials stated that raising the weapons question before Phase 1 is complete is unacceptable. This position stalls the Board of Peace 8-month disarmament proposal that was submitted to Hamas on March 24, 2026, which called for gradual disarmament with five principles: reciprocity, sequencing, verification, reintegration/amnesty, and timeline flexibility. Hamas had counter-proposed a 3-year timeline allowing retention of light arms.

media.caption: Hamas Refuses Disarmament Talks Until Israel Fulfills Phase 1 Obligations

title: US Senators Request Briefing on Joint Task Force October 7 Progress
detail: US Senators Katie Britt (R-AL), Jerry Moran (R-KS), and colleagues sent a formal letter in early April 2026 to Attorney General Pam Bondi and FBI Director Kash Patel requesting a comprehensive briefing on the Joint Task Force October 7 (JTF), which was established to hold perpetrators of the October 7 attack legally accountable. The senators sought updates on prosecutorial actions against Hamas operatives, asset seizures, and cooperation with Israeli law enforcement. JTF October 7 has focused on US citizens killed or taken hostage and foreign nationals with US ties. As of April 2026, no Hamas operatives have been publicly indicted in US courts.

sources.name: Senator Katie Britt — Official Press Release
